column1: # GIGANTIC upload_manifest.tsv — phylonames STEP_1 generate_and_evaluate template
# When workflow-COPYME-generate_phylonames is copied to workflow-RUN_N-generate_phylonames,
# this manifest travels with it and describes which terminal files from
# OUTPUT_pipeline/N-output/ get published to the project's data server.
# The surrounding hierarchy (subproject / step / workflow) is INFERRED from
# the manifest's location by the shared helper at
# gigantic_project-COPYME/server/ai/update_upload_to_server.py.
# You only describe files in this manifest.
# COLUMNS (TAB-separated — mind the tab count; header row REQUIRED):
#   source_path     Path to file(s). Relative to this manifest's directory.
#                   Globs allowed (e.g., OUTPUT_pipeline/4-output/*.html).
#   include         yes | no  — toggle publication.
#   dest_name       (optional) Override the filename in upload_to_server/.
#                   Blank = use source basename.
#   display_label   (optional) Human-readable title shown in the web UI.
#                   Blank = use filename.
#   file_category   (optional) Grouping label. Files in the same category
#                   are grouped under the same subsection.
#                   Suggested for phylonames: summary, mapping, log.
#   description     (optional) One-line tooltip / subtitle.
#   order           (optional) Integer sort key within category (default 100).
#                   Lower = earlier.
source_path
OUTPUT_pipeline/4-output/*_taxonomy_summary.html
column6: Human-readable per-species taxonomy summary generated from NCBI taxonomy.

column1: OUTPUT_pipeline/4-output/*_taxonomy_summary.md
column6: Markdown version of the per-species taxonomy summary.

column1: OUTPUT_pipeline/3-output/*_map-genus_species_X_phylonames.tsv
column6: Canonical mapping file. Flip to yes to publish; usually downstream subprojects consume it via output_to_input/ rather than fetching from the server.

column1: OUTPUT_pipeline/5-output/5_ai-run_log.txt
column6: Per-run audit log. Flip to yes if you want collaborators to see the run record.
